mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in real retail and food services sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return is not positively correlated with RSAFS month-over-month change at a rank IC of at least 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals a weakening labor market and impending recession, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as earnings expectations fall.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.5% within the last 3 months
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE crosses 4.5%, the subsequent 20-day SPX return is not negative in more than 55% of cases, or the average excess return is not less than -0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high inflation (CPIAUCSL year-over-year > 3%) and low unemployment (UNRATE < 4%) indicate an overheating economy that forces the central bank to tighten policy aggressively, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: When both conditions hold simultaneously, the 20-day SPX return is not negative in at least 60% of observations, or the negative signal fails to predict returns beyond a buy-and-hold baseline in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, elevated housing starts (HOUST) represent speculative overinvestment that will be corrected as credit tightens, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: In defined tightening periods (FEDFUNDS > 3% and rising over 3 months), the rank IC of HOUST on 60-day forward returns is not below -0.05 or the long-short decile spread is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals immediate labor market weakness that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS prints negative monthly growth, the average 5-day forward return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) or the hit rate is below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in both industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals a synchronized contraction in supply and demand, confirming an economic downturn that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month negative and RSAFS month-over-month negative
failure_condition: When both series contract simultaneously, the 20-day SPX return is not negative in more than 55% of instances, or the signal does not improve upon a simple trend-following strategy.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects a robust aggregate demand environment that supports corporate profits and equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The rank IC of GDPC1 quarterly change on 60-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 10-year window, or the predictive R-squared from a univariate regression is less than 1%.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment (CPIAUCSL year-over-year below 2%), a low unemployment rate (UNRATE below 4%) signals a healthy labor market with no overheating pressures, supporting consumer confidence and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short-term horizon.
failure_condition: In periods where inflation is below 2% and unemployment below 4%, the 5-day SPX return is not positive in more than 55% of cases, or the average return is not significantly above the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).
